summary:
There is significant discordance between automated analyzer reports and manual microscopic evaluations of red blood cell morphology, particularly regarding detection of specific inclusions such as Heinz bodies and Hemoglobin H. Observer variability among experienced clinical pathologists further contributes to discrepancies in interpretation despite shared expertise.
